exercises.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

items.sentence: We should ...... our train tickets before they become more expensive.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Book tickets = reserve them in advance.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: You need a valid ...... to travel to another country.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: You need a passport to cross borders.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The train to London is leaving from ...... 7.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Trains leave from a numbered platform.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The airport was very ...... because it was the start of the holidays.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is lots of people, so the correct answer is "crowded".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence: There was so much ...... on the motorway that we missed our ferry.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Heavy traffic on the motorway caused the delay.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: Please don't leave your ...... unattended at the station.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Don't leave your luggage unattended.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Our final ...... is a quiet village in the mountains.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Your final destination is where the journey ends.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: Have you ever studied ...... for more than a month?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Study abroad = in another country.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

exercises.title: Circle the correct word
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.originalSentence: We stayed in comfortable **accommodation / luggage** near the harbour.
items.completedSentence: We stayed in comfortable accommodation near the harbour.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "accommodation" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "accommodation" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "luggage" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.luggage: "luggage" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("near the harbour.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: from Madrid to Lisbon took six hours.
items.originalSentence: The **journey / passport** from Madrid to Lisbon took six hours.
items.completedSentence: The journey from Madrid to Lisbon took six hours.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "journey" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "journey" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "passport" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.passport: "passport" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("from Madrid to Lisbon took six hours.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: several flights because of the storm.
items.originalSentence: The airline had to **cancel / catch** several flights because of the storm.
items.completedSentence: The airline had to cancel several flights because of the storm.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "cancel" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "cancel" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "catch" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.catch: "catch" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("several flights because of the storm.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: Run! We can still **catch / crash** the coach if we hurry.
items.completedSentence: Run! We can still catch the coach if we hurry.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "catch" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "catch" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "crash" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.crash: "crash" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("the coach if we hurry.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: A bicycle is a useful **vehicle / resort** for short journeys.
items.completedSentence: A bicycle is a useful vehicle for short journeys.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "vehicle" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "vehicle" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "resort" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.resort: "resort" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("for short journeys.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: because it's next to the station.
items.originalSentence: This hotel is more **convenient / foreign** because it's next to the station.
items.completedSentence: This hotel is more convenient because it's next to the station.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "convenient" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "convenient" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "foreign" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.foreign: "foreign" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("because it's next to the station.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

exercises.title: Phrasal verbs (complete the gaps)
exercises.instructions: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.

items.sentence: We should ...... before sunrise if we want to reach the coast by lunch.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Set off = start a journey.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "set off" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "We should ...... before sunrise if we want to reach the coast by lunch.".

items.sentence: The bus is here! Let's ...... before it leaves.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Get on the bus = board it.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: You need to ...... at the next stop and change trains.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Get off at the next stop = leave the train.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: I couldn't ...... the taxi because the door was stuck.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Get out of the taxi = leave it.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: The plane is going to ...... in five minutes.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Planes take off = leave the ground.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: We had such a good time that we didn't want to ...... home.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Go back home = return home.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

exercises.title: Prepositional phrases (matching)
exercises.instructions: Match sentence beginnings with the correct ending.
exercises.studentInstruction: Match sentence beginnings with the correct ending.

items.sentence: They travelled across Europe **D** by train.
Passengers must remain seated **F** on board.
We visited the old city centre **B** on foot.
My cousins are **E** on holiday.
Luckily, the coach arrived **A** on schedule.
Their apartment is **C** on the coast.

exercises.title: Word patterns (adjectives and verbs)
exercises.instructions: Write one preposition in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one preposition in each gap.

items.sentence: The airport is close ...... our hotel.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Close to = near our hotel.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: This island is famous ...... its beautiful beaches.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Famous for its beaches (fixed pattern).
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The campsite is far ...... the nearest town.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Far from the nearest town.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: We were late ...... our flight because of traffic.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Late for our flight (not late to).
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: I asked the guide ...... the history of the castle.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Ask about the history of the castle.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: We waited ...... the ferry for over an hour.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Wait for the ferry.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: The hotel provided us ...... clean towels every day.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Provide someone with towels.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

exercises.title: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The old town is the main tourist ...... in the area. (ATTRACT)
Can you give me ...... to the nearest underground station? (DIRECT)
Our ...... from London was at seven in the morning. (DEPART)
The bus ...... was very friendly and helped us with our bags. (DRIVE)
The ...... from Athens to Rome was delayed. (FLY)
A good ...... always checks local customs before visiting a country. (TRAVEL)
The hotel is popular with ...... from all over the world. (VISIT)
The seats on the coach were really ...... . (COMFORT)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: We reached the resort = arrived at.

wrongOptions.B: "caught" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.
wrongOptions.caught: "caught" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "booked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.
wrongOptions.booked: "booked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.
wrongOptions.D: "packed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.
wrongOptions.packed: "packed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "reached" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links past actions to now — look for markers like for, since, already, yet, or ever.

items.sentenceAfter: you with all the information you need.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The receptionist will provide you with information.

wrongOptions.A: "ask" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.
wrongOptions.ask: "ask" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "wait" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.
wrongOptions.wait: "wait" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.
wrongOptions.D: "arrive" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.
wrongOptions.arrive: "arrive" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "provide" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Future forms (will, going to, present continuous) depend on whether the sentence predicts, plans, or arranges.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Suitable for families (fixed pattern).

wrongOptions.A: "to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.
wrongOptions.to: "to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.
wrongOptions.B: "with" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.
wrongOptions.with: "with" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.
wrongOptions.D: "about" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.
wrongOptions.about: "about" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "for" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "for" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "by road" — airport closed.

wrongOptions.on board: "on board" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "by road" is correct.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: A ferry leaves from a harbour.

exercises.title: Mixed revision
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with one suitable word.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences with one suitable word.

items.sentence: Let's have a short ...... before we continue driving.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Have a short break = pause during a drive.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The museum is near here, so we can go on ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Go on foot = walk.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Our hotel was far ...... the city centre.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Far from the city centre.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Don't forget to ...... your suitcase tonight.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Pack your suitcase before the trip.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: We got ...... the train at the wrong station.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Got off the train = left it.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Can you ...... at the timetable and tell me the departure time?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Look at the timetable to find the time.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: The flight left ...... schedule.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: On schedule = on time.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: I bought a small ...... to remember my trip to Venice.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: A souvenir reminds you of a trip.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.
